Response: Istituto Italiano di Tecnologia (IIT), Genoa, Italy; RobotCub Consortium (EU-funded iCub humanoid robot project); primary developers: Lorenzo Natale, Daniele Pagnin, and IIT Robotics team; originates from humanoid robot research (iCub)

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: Open-source middleware for humanoid and general robotics; provides communication infrastructure for distributed robot systems (ports, carriers, protocols); supports inter-process communication between robot sensor and actuator modules; includes libraries for sensor processing, motor control, computer vision, and device abstraction; primary middleware for the iCub humanoid robot platform and used in many academic humanoid robot projects

column4: YARP is the primary middleware for the iCub humanoid robot — one of the world's most widely used open humanoid platforms (worldwide network of 40+ iCub labs). YARP's port-based communication model provides device abstraction enabling transparency between simulated (Gazebo) and real iCub. YARP has been continuously developed and maintained by IIT since 2006. The robotology superbuild (https://github.com/robotology/robotology-superbuild) provides a comprehensive installation framework for YARP and all IIT robotics software.
